name: Irish Brown Bread #2 Monica Sheridan
recipeIngredient: 4 cups Stone Ground Whole Wheat Flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the whole wheat flour thoroughly with the white flour in a large bowl.
2. Rub the butter into the flours until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Add the salt, baking soda, and sugar; mix well to combine.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients and gradually stir in the liquid (buttermilk or milk) with a wooden spoon. Adjust the amount of liquid as needed based on flour absorbency. The dough should be soft but manageable.
5. Using floured hands, knead the dough into a ball in the bowl.
6. Place the dough on a lightly floured surface and shape it into a round (1 1/2 inches thick).
7. Make a cross cut through the center of the bread with a knife dipped in flour to allow steam to escape during baking.
8. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 25 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 350°F (175°C) and bake an additional 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
9. If the crust is too hard, wrap the bread loosely in a damp tea towel and let it cool completely before slicing.
10. Allow the bread to stand upright until it has cooled fully, about 6 hours after baking. Slice and serve.
